message:
fix(tui): prevent UTF-8 panics in string truncation across all screens

Every `truncate_str` / `compact_path` helper was slicing on byte
offsets (`s[..n]`), which panics when `n` falls inside a multi-byte
UTF-8 sequence. This is trivially triggered by agent names, file
paths, or message subjects containing CJK characters, arrows (→),
or emoji.

All affected sites now use `.chars().count()` for length checks and
`.chars().take(n).collect()` for truncation, which always splits on
character boundaries.

Screens fixed:
- console.rs: render_http_request_panel path/IP truncation, compact_path
- attachments.rs: subject truncation in attachment explorer
- contacts.rs: truncate_str helper
- explorer.rs: truncate_str helper
- messages.rs: truncate_str helper
- search.rs: truncate_str helper (inferred — same pattern)
- threads.rs: truncate_str helper (inferred — same pattern)
- scope_policy_property.rs: adversarial unicode agent name assertion

Each fix includes comprehensive test coverage with ASCII, 2-byte
(é), 3-byte (→, CJK), 4-byte (emoji), and mixed-multibyte sweep
inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
